text: Book, which Joakim burnt, and sought the life of the prophets, Jeremias 36. (Calmet)<hr>Baruch apprised of the general distress, weeps for it, and his life is spared. (Worthington)

Great: the spirit of prophecy; (Rabbins) or rather to be exempted from suffering, while all the neighbouring nations were perishing. (Calmet)<hr>And save. Literally, "for salvation." Hebrew, "booty." (Haydock)<hr>He seems to have been set at liberty with his master, Jeremias 40:1. (Calmet)
